content.full_text:
San Bernandino County                                    Superintendent of Schools
601 North E Street ● San Bernardino, CA 92410-3093    Charles S. Terrell, Jr., Superintendent ● (714) 387-4386


December 19, 1990


Mr. Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA    95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

As stipulated in my November 26, 1990 letter, I have received formal notification from the California Franchise Tax Board, that my application #344:9:BC for the Charles S. Terrell, Jr. Scholarship Foundation has been authorized as an educational charitable organization. Enclosed is the December 13, 1990 letter from B. Clark, Exempt Organization, General Audit, acknowledging its authorization.

The Articles of Incorporation for the Charles S. Terrell, Jr. Scholarship Foundation have been filed with the Secretary of State.

My account (Tax I.D. #33-0049717) at Life Savings Bank, 1598 E. Highland Avenue, San Bernardino, Committee to Re-elect Chuck Terrell, will be transferred into the newly authorized Charles S. Terrell, Jr. Scholarship Foundation.

Sincerely, yours,

[signature]
Charles S. Terrell, Jr.
County Superintendent of Schools

Enclosure
CST:AR:mtc

cc: B. Clark, Exempt Organization
    General Audit, Franchise Tax Board


Successes from the past … Looking to the future

STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FRANCHISE TAX BOARD
P. O. BOX 651
SACRAMENTO, CA 95812-0651

December 13, 1990

In reply refer to
344:G  :BC


CHARLES S. TERRELL JR. SCHOLARSHIP
FOUNDATION
3730 OSBUN RD
SN BERNARDINO CA  92404


Purpose                     :  CHARITABLE, EDUCATIONAL
Code Section                :  23701d
Form of Organization        :  Corporation
Accounting Period Ending :  September 30
Organization Number         :


You are exempt from state franchise or income tax under the section of
the Revenue and Taxation Code indicated above.

This decision is based on information you submitted and assumes that
your present operations continue unchanged or conform to those proposed
in your application.  Any change in operation, character, or purpose of
the organization must be reported immediately to this office so that we
may determine the effect on your exempt status.  Any change of name or
address also must be reported.

In the event of a change in relevant statutory, administrative, judicial
case law, a change in federal interpretation of federal law in cases
where our opinion is based upon such an interpretation, or a change in
the material facts or circumstances relating to your application upon
which this opinion is based, this opinion may no longer be applicable.
It is your responsibility to be aware of these changes should they occur.
This paragraph constitutes written advice, other than a chief counsel
ruling, within the meaning of Revenue and Taxation Code Section
21012(a)(2).

You may be required to file Form 199 ( Exempt Organization Annual
Information Return) on or before the 15th day of the 5th month (4 1/2
months) after the close of your accounting period.  See annual
instructions with forms for requirements.

You are not required to file state franchise or income tax returns
unless you have income subject to the unrelated business income tax
under Section 23731 of the Code.  In this event, you are required to
file Form 109 (Exempt Organization Business Income Tax Return) by the
15th day of the 5th month (4 1/2 months) after the close of your
annual accounting period.

December 13, 1990
CHARLES S. TERRELL JR. SCHOLARSHIP
Page 2


If the organization is incorporating, this approval will expire unless
incorporation is completed with the Secretary of State within 60 days.

Exemption from federal income or other taxes and other state taxes
requires separate applications.

A copy of this letter has been sent to the Office of the Secretary of
State and to the Registry of Charitable Trusts.

B CLARK
EXEMPT ORGANIZATION
GENERAL AUDIT
Telephone (916) 369-4171

EO :
cc: KENNETH E. SKOUSEN




COPY

San Bernardi no County                                                                    Superintendent of Schools
601 North E Street ● San Bernardino, CA 92410-3093                    Charles S. Terrell, Jr., Superintendent ● (714) 387-4386


November 26, 1990


Mr. Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Ref:  Application #344:APL:BC

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This will confirm my recent telephone conversation with your
office representative concerning my application #344:APL:BC
for the Charles S. Terrell, Jr. Scholarship Foundation with
the Franchise Tax Board, State of California. As discussed,
my account #191100189 (TAX I.D. #33-0049717) at Life Savings
Bank, 1598 East Highland Avenue, San Bernardino, California,
92404, Committee to Re-elect Chuck Terrell, San Bernardino
County Superintendent of Schools, 3730 Osbun Road, San Bernardi-
no, California, was internally transferred on October 17,
1990 (same bank) to a fixed rate - fixed term 6-month
certificate, account #19152119-4.

My application to the State Franchise Tax Board is being pro-
cessed by B. Clark, Exempt Organization General Audit, State
of California Franchise Tax Board, P.O. Box 651, Sacramento,
CA  95812-0651.

I hope this will comply with any notification requirements to
your office. I will inform your office when my Charles S.
Terrell, Jr. Scholarship Foundation application has been
approved.

Sincerely yours,

[signature]

Charles S. Terrell, Jr.
County Superintendent of Schools

CST:AR:mtc


Successes from the past … Looking to the future
